source: AGP-11 lutherie plan by Stoll E. Artus, 1983. 3-sheet full-size construction drawing. Reference instrument: Epiphone Spartan Ser. No. 2431, Epiphone NY.
description: The Epiphone Spartan was an entry-level archtop guitar produced by Epiphone in New York during the 1930s-40s. Features carved spruce top, maple back and sides, single f-holes, floating pickguard, and trapeze tailpiece. Part of Epiphone's pre-Gibson archtop lineup alongside the Triumph, Broadway, and Emperor models.

body.note: Dimensions estimated from AGP-11 full-size plan. 16-inch lower bout class archtop.

f_holes.note: Traditional f-hole shape, positioned in upper bout area

top_arching.description: Arches shown are similar top & back (per Sheet 3 note)
top_arching.note: Graduation and arching patterns shown in Sheet 3

neck.note: Fret positions table visible in Sheet 1

headstock.note: Classic Epiphone 'open book' headstock shape shown in Sheet 1

bridge.note: Floating bridge with rosewood base, height-adjustable wheels

tailpiece.assembly_detail: Sheet 3 - Tailpiece Assembly (chrome or nickel plated, with rosewood insert block)

pickguard.bracket_detail: Sheet 3 - Pick Guard Mounting Bracket (chrome or nickel plated steel)
pickguard.note: Elevated pickguard with side-mounted bracket, see Sheet 2 and Sheet 3

label.text: This Epiphone Instrument is Model 'Spartan' Ser. No. 2431
label.manufacturer_address: Epiphone Inc., New York, N.Y., U.S.A.

construction_notes: See Sheet 3 for details of pickguard mounting brackets
Arches shown are similar top & back (Sheet 3 header note)
Tailpiece assembly: chrome or nickel plated with rosewood insert
Pick guard mounting bracket - on soundboard (Sheet 3)
Pick guard mounting bracket - on neck (Sheet 3)
Finish of entire instrument is 'Shaded' Sunburst

finish.description: Shaded sunburst - amber center fading to dark brown edges
finish.source: Sheet 2 note: 'Finish of entire instrument is Shaded Sunburst'

historical_notes: The Spartan was Epiphone's entry-level archtop, below the Blackstone in the lineup
Produced during Epiphone's New York era (1930s-1950s)
Reference instrument Ser. No. 2431 dates to late 1930s production
Plan AGP-11 by Stoll E. Artus is a professional lutherie reference
cam_notes: Plan images available but not yet vectorized. Archtop carving requires 3D CAM capability.
